info.title: API Reference
info.description: Custom document-processing APIs powered by PDF Vector

post.summary: Ask a question about a document
post.description: Parse a document and answer a question about its content using AI. Supports PDF, Word (.docx), Excel (.xlsx), CSV, and Image (.png, .jpg) files. Files up to 1000 pages and up to 500MB in size. Provide the document via file upload, a public URL, or a base64-encoded string.

url.description: URL of the document file to fetch and parse

file.description: Document file upload via multipart form-data (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, CSV, PNG, JPG)

question.description: The question to answer about the document

model.description: Model tier for answering the question. 'auto' (default): Automatically selects the best tier based on document page count and document complexity. 'nano': Uses lightweight models (likely better than GPT-5-nano). Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV. 'mini': Uses mid-range models (likely better than GPT-5-mini). Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV. 'pro': Uses capable models (likely better than GPT-5.2). Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV, Image. 'max': Uses the most powerful models (likely better than Claude Opus 4.6). Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV, Image.

value.question: What are the main findings of this study?
What is the content of this document?
What is the title of this paper?
Provide a detailed analysis of the methodology used in this study.

'pro': Uses capable models (likely better than GPT-5.2). Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV, Image. 'max': Uses the most powerful models (likely better than Claude Opus 4.6). Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV, Image.

parameters.description: Optional document ID to associate with this request. Returned in the response and saved for usage tracking.

post.summary: Extract structured data from a document
post.description: Parse a document and extract structured data matching a provided JSON Schema using AI. Supports PDF, Word (.docx), Excel (.xlsx), CSV, and Image (.png, .jpg) files. Files up to 1000 pages and up to 500MB in size. Provide the document via file upload, a public URL, or a base64-encoded string.

url.description: URL of the document file to fetch and parse

file.description: Document file upload via multipart form-data (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, CSV, PNG, JPG)

prompt.description: The prompt instructing the AI how to extract data from the document

schema.description: JSON Schema describing the structure of the data to extract from the document. Can be a JSON object or a JSON string.

model.description: Model tier for extracting structured data. 'auto' (default): Automatically selects the best tier based on document page count and document complexity. 'nano': Uses lightweight models (likely better than GPT-5-nano). Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV. 'mini': Uses mid-range models (likely better than GPT-5-mini). Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV. 'pro': Uses capable models (likely better than GPT-5.2). Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV, Image. 'max': Uses the most powerful models (likely better than Claude Opus 4.6). Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV, Image.

post.description: Extract text and page count from a document. Supports PDF, Word (.docx), Excel (.xlsx), CSV, and Image (.png, .jpg) files. Provide the document via file upload, a public URL, or a base64-encoded string.

url.description: URL of the document file to fetch and parse

file.description: Document file upload via multipart form-data (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, CSV, PNG, JPG)

model.description: Model tier for parsing. 'auto' (default): Automatically selects the best parsing strategy with intelligent fallback. File up to 1000 pages, up to 500MB in size. Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV, Image. 'nano': For simple documents with plain text content. File up to 30 pages, up to 10MB in size. Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV. 'mini': For documents with tables and structured content. File up to 30 pages, up to 10MB in size. Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV. 'pro': For documents up to 30 pages with tables, handwritten text, figures, math, and Arabic. File up to 30 pages, up to 40MB in size. Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV, Image. 'max': For large documents with full Pro capabilities plus enhanced multilingual support. File up to 1000 pages, up to 500MB in size. Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV, Image.

url.description: URL of the document file to fetch and parse

file.description: Document file upload via multipart form-data (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, CSV, PNG, JPG)

model.description: Model tier for parsing. 'auto' (default): Automatically selects the best parsing strategy with intelligent fallback. File up to 1000 pages, up to 500MB in size. Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV, Image. 'nano': For simple documents with plain text content. File up to 30 pages, up to 10MB in size. Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV. 'mini': For documents with tables and structured content. File up to 30 pages, up to 10MB in size. Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV. 'pro': For documents up to 30 pages with tables, handwritten text, figures, math, and Arabic. File up to 30 pages, up to 40MB in size. Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV, Image. 'max': For large documents with full Pro capabilities plus enhanced multilingual support. File up to 1000 pages, up to 500MB in size. Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV, Image.

markdown.description: Extracted text content from the document

pageCount.description: Total number of pages in the document

model.description: Model tier used to parse the document. 'nano': Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV. 'mini': Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV. 'pro': Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV, Image. 'max': Supports PDF, Word, Excel, CSV, Image.

requestId.description: Unique request identifier for this API call

html.description: Full HTML representation of the document content. Only available when using the 'max' model. Preserves rich formatting, tables, selection marks, and visual layout that cannot be fully represented in markdown.

documentId.description: Document ID if provided via x-pdfvector-document-id header

parameters.description: Optional document ID to associate with this request. Returned in the response and saved for usage tracking.

post.description: Fetch specific academic publications by their unique identifiers with automatic provider detection and batch processing.

ids.description: Publication identifiers to fetch. Supports DOI, PubMed ID, ArXiv ID, Semantic Scholar ID, ERIC ID, Europe PMC ID, OpenAlex ID.

fields.description: Specific publication fields to return. If omitted, returns all fields except providerData.

Fetch multiple IDs.summary: Fetch papers by mixed identifier types

post.description: Find relevant academic citations for a paragraph by splitting it into sentences and scoring papers using semantic embedding similarity.

paragraph.description: Text paragraph to find relevant academic citations for

providers.description: Academic databases to search for citations

fields.description: Specific citation fields to return. If omitted, returns all fields except providerData.

Find citations for a paragraph.summary: Find citations for a research paragraph

value.paragraph: Deep learning has revolutionized computer vision and natural language processing. Transformer architectures have become the dominant approach for sequence modeling tasks. Recent large language models demonstrate emergent capabilities not seen in smaller models.
CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing technology has transformed molecular biology. The ability to precisely modify DNA sequences has opened new avenues for treating genetic diseases.

post.description: Search for academic publications across multiple databases with relevance ranking.

query.description: Search query string for academic papers

yearFrom.description: Filter papers published from this year

yearTo.description: Filter papers published up to this year

fields.description: Specific publication fields to return. If omitted, returns all fields except providerData.

value.query: deep learning for natural language processing
